description: OpenClaw channel plugin for claw-msg agent-to-agent messaging. Modeled after the Matrix plugin.

userStories.title: Plugin manifest and package setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the plugin project scaffolded with openclaw.plugin.json, package.json, tsconfig, and index.ts so that OpenClaw can discover and load it.
acceptanceCriteria: openclaw.plugin.json exists with id 'claw-msg', channels: ['claw-msg']
package.json has name 'claw-msg', openclaw.extensions pointing to ./index.ts, channel metadata (id, label, blurb, order), and install hints
tsconfig.json configured for ESM TypeScript
index.ts exports default plugin object with id, name, description, register(api) that calls api.registerChannel
Dependencies: ws (WebSocket client)
userStories.notes: Follow Matrix plugin structure exactly. Use openclaw/plugin-sdk/core for types. Broker default URL: https://jiho-system-product-name.tail1de967.ts.net/claw

userStories.title: Config schema and account resolution
userStories.description: As a user, I want to configure claw-msg in openclaw.json with broker URL, token, name, and dmPolicy so that the plugin knows how to connect.
acceptanceCriteria: config-schema.ts defines Zod schema for claw-msg channel config: broker (string, default broker URL), token (optional string), name (string), dmPolicy (open|contacts_only, default open)
accounts.ts resolves account config — single account or multi-account pattern
Config validation works without executing plugin code (schema-only)
Default broker URL is https://jiho-system-product-name.tail1de967.ts.net/claw

userStories.title: WebSocket monitor — connect, auth, heartbeat, receive
userStories.description: As a plugin, I want to maintain a persistent WebSocket connection to the claw-msg broker so that I can receive messages in real-time.
acceptanceCriteria: monitor.ts connects to broker WebSocket endpoint (ws://broker/ws)
Sends auth frame with bearer token on connect
Handles auth.ok and auth.fail responses
Responds to ping frames with pong
Receives message.receive frames and dispatches to inbound handler
Auto-reconnects with exponential backoff on disconnect
Cleanly disconnects on shutdown
userStories.notes: Protocol frames: auth, auth.ok, auth.fail, message.receive, message.send, message.ack, ping, pong, error. Use ws library for WebSocket client.

userStories.title: Auto-registration on first start
userStories.description: As a user, I want the plugin to automatically register with the broker if no token is configured so that I don't need to manually run claw-msg register.
acceptanceCriteria: On plugin load, if config has no token: POST /agents/register to broker with agent name
Save received token to openclaw.json config automatically
Use agent name from config, falling back to OpenClaw agent name
Log registration success with agent_id
On subsequent starts, use saved token directly
userStories.notes: Registration endpoint: POST /agents/register body: {name, capabilities, metadata}. Response: {agent_id, token}

userStories.title: Inbound message routing
userStories.description: As an OpenClaw agent, I want to receive claw-msg messages as normal inbound messages with metadata indicating the sender is an agent.
acceptanceCriteria: inbound.ts converts claw-msg message.receive frames to OpenClaw inbound format
Inbound message includes: channel='claw-msg', sender=agent_name, sender_id='claw-msg:<agent-id>'
Metadata includes: source='claw-msg', agent_id, agent_name, is_agent=true, reply_to (if present)
Message is routed to the correct OpenClaw agent via bindings
Agent can respond naturally and response triggers outbound flow
userStories.notes: Use api.registerChannel's onMessage callback pattern from Matrix plugin

userStories.title: Outbound message sending
userStories.description: As an OpenClaw agent, I want to send messages to other agents via the message tool with channel=claw-msg.
acceptanceCriteria: outbound.ts handles send requests from OpenClaw message tool
Sends message via WebSocket if connected (message.send frame)
Falls back to HTTP POST /messages/ if WebSocket not available
Target is resolved from agent_id or contact alias
Returns delivery status

userStories.title: Channel plugin interface — full ChannelPlugin implementation
userStories.description: As an OpenClaw gateway, I want claw-msg to implement the ChannelPlugin interface so it integrates like any other channel.
acceptanceCriteria: channel.ts implements ChannelPlugin with all required methods
start() connects to broker via monitor
stop() disconnects cleanly
send() routes to outbound
Channel appears in openclaw channels list and openclaw status
Pairing/allowlist works for claw-msg senders
userStories.notes: Follow Matrix channel.ts structure. This is the main integration point.
